message:
test(ft-xbnl0.2.4): cert-file + min-TLS-version error-path contracts (tick 335)

Added two tests pinning error-variant fidelity for common operator
mis-configurations:

- `build_tls_bundle_surfaces_io_error_with_path_for_missing_cert_file`
  — nonexistent cert path produces `DistributedTlsError::Io { path, source }`
  with the offending path in the struct field (not just in the Display
  string). Pins that automated caller-side error handling can key on
  the variant and extract the path without parsing the Display output.

- `resolve_tls_versions_rejects_unsupported_version_string` — min TLS
  version string `"2.0"` (not a real TLS version) fails with
  `InvalidMinTlsVersion("2.0")`. The carried string must match the
  input so operator-facing error messages can echo back what was
  configured.

Discovery during development: `resolve_tls_versions` is called AFTER
`load_cert_chain` in `build_server_config`, so a config with
`/dev/null` for cert/key and a bad version string fails with
`EmptyCertChain("/dev/null")` before the version check fires. The
test fix uses real PEM fixtures (SERVER_CERT / SERVER_KEY constants
already in the test module) so the version-check path is actually
reached. Noted in test comment for future readers.

Test result: 2 passed; 0 failed
Compile time: 1m 16s cold + 1.26s incremental for the first test
rerun.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
